note: Agent context kit — reuse a component before inventing. use_when from usage-doctrine.yaml.

components.name: Accordion
use_when: FAQ-style breakdowns in a report (methodology, data sources, limitations as stacked sections)
Grouping insight categories where users scan headers and expand one at a time (type="single" collapsible)
Settings pages with multiple bordered sections sharing identical trigger styling
dont_use_when: A single expandable region or custom trigger layout — use Collapsible
Sections users compare side by side or switch between constantly — use Tabs
Hiding rarely-used configuration behind one labeled toggle — use AdvancedSection

components.name: Alert
use_when: Inline feedback within a page (form errors, plan limits, info)
Persistent contextual messages that live in the content
dont_use_when: Ephemeral feedback after an action — use Toast
Blocking actions — use AlertDialog

components.name: Alert Dialog
use_when: Confirming deletion of a space, feed, or saved report where data loss is irreversible
Confirming a destructive bulk action on signals (e.g. dismiss all, purge feed items)
Interrupting the user before an action that consumes credits or triggers an external send
dont_use_when: Non-blocking editing or configuration of a feed or report — use Dialog
Lightweight inline confirmation that can be undone — use a toast with undo action instead
Contextual info or settings anchored to a trigger — use Popover

components.name: Aspect Ratio
use_when: Locking media in signal cards to a consistent ratio (post thumbnails, ad creatives at 16/9 or 1/1)
Embedded video or chart previews in reports that must keep proportions across responsive grids
dont_use_when: Content whose height should follow its text — use Card with natural flow
Square avatars — use Avatar, which handles sizing and fallback

components.name: Avatar
use_when: Representing a person (round shape)
Representing a brand/company/logo (square shape)
Member lists, author attribution, team displays
dont_use_when: Status indicators or labels — use Badge
Decorative icons that don't represent a person or brand — use Icon

components.name: Badge
use_when: Status labels (Active, Paused, Error, Draft)
Counts and metadata tags
Category labels on cards
dont_use_when: Interactive actions — use Button
Long text — badges are single-word or short phrase only

components.name: Breadcrumb
use_when: Showing location depth inside a space hierarchy (Space > Feed > Signal detail)
Report drill-downs where users navigate back up the path
Long paths that need truncation — BreadcrumbEllipsis collapses middle ancestors
dont_use_when: Top-level section switching — use Sidebar or Tabs; breadcrumbs reflect position, they don't offer peers
A single back action — use a Button with a chevron instead of a one-segment breadcrumb

components.name: Button
use_when: Primary action in a view (one per screen max for default variant)
Form submission, dialog confirmation, CTA
dont_use_when: Navigation between pages — use a link instead
Destructive first-touch actions — use destructive variant only for confirmation

components.name: Calendar
use_when: Picking a custom date range for a signals or mentions feed (range selection inside a Popover)
Scheduling a report delivery date or a space digest start date
Filtering insights by a specific day or week in a brand timeline view
dont_use_when: Choosing among preset periods like "Last 7 days / 30 days" — use SegmentedControl or Select
Picking a single year or month from a long list — use Select
Displaying read-only date metadata on a signal card — plain text, no picker needed

components.name: Card
use_when: Grouping a self-contained insight or report summary on a dashboard, with title, description, and an action in the header
Presenting a signal feed's stat block (mention counts, sentiment deltas) as a bounded surface
Wrapping a space overview tile (brand logo, latest activity, quick actions) in a grid of spaces
dont_use_when: Rendering a long uniform list of signals or mentions — use ListItem inside a plain container
Showing dense comparative data across feeds — use Table
Housing secondary or power-user settings — use AdvancedSection

components.name: Carousel
use_when: Swiping through ad creatives or post media attached to a single signal
Browsing a horizontal strip of featured insight cards on a space home
Stepping through report cover previews in a gallery
dont_use_when: Listing many signals that all need to be scannable at once — use a grid or Table
Step-by-step onboarding or setup flows — use WizardDialog
Two or three items that fit the viewport — lay them out side by side

components.name: Checkbox
use_when: Multi-select from a list
Boolean toggle that needs a label
Settings that apply independently
dont_use_when: Mutually exclusive options — use RadioGroup
Immediate action toggle — use Switch

components.name: Collapsible
use_when: Show/hide of a single optional region (expanding a truncated transcript, "see all 12 sources")
Collapsible sub-groups inside the Sidebar (pairs with SidebarMenuSub)
One-off "more details" toggles where multiple regions may be open simultaneously
dont_use_when: A stack of related expandable sections with shared styling — use Accordion
Advanced or destructive settings that warrant a styled, labeled reveal — use AdvancedSection

components.name: Combobox
use_when: Typeahead brand picker when adding brands to track in a space
Selecting a feed or report destination from a long list while typing to filter
Tag/topic input on insights where options are fetched or numerous
dont_use_when: Few options (under ~7) with no search need — use Select
Full-screen navigational search — use Command in a palette
Free-form text with no constrained options — use Input

components.name: Command
use_when: Global command palette (Cmd+K) to jump between spaces, feeds, and reports
Searchable action list inside a Popover (e.g. assign signal to feed from many options)
Filterable multi-group pickers — brands, sources, report templates — with keyboard navigation
dont_use_when: Short static option list bound to a form value — use Select
Plain action menus with no search need — use DropdownMenu
Single-field autocomplete bound to an input — use Combobox

components.name: Context Menu
use_when: Right-click actions on a signal card in a feed (pin, mute brand, copy link, add to report)
Right-click on a space or feed in the sidebar tree (rename, duplicate, archive)
Power-user shortcuts on report blocks or insight rows without adding visible chrome
dont_use_when: Actions that need a discoverable visible trigger — use DropdownMenu on a kebab/ellipsis button
Persistent app-level menus — use Menubar
Picking a value for a form field — use Select or Combobox

components.name: Data Table
use_when: Displaying a list of brands, mentions, or campaigns where the user needs to sort, filter, or paginate
Any tabular data set with more than ~15 rows where filtering by a column is useful
Tables that need column-visibility toggling (e.g. dashboard config views)
dont_use_when: Simple read-only tables with ≤10 fixed rows — use Table directly
Single-column lists — use ListView / ListItem
Leaderboard-style ranked rows — use LeaderboardRow

components.name: Date Picker
use_when: Selecting a single date for scheduling (report delivery, digest start date, export date)
Selecting a date range for filtering signals, mentions, or brand timeline data
dont_use_when: Choosing preset periods like "Last 7 days / 30 days" — use SegmentedControl or Select
Showing a read-only date on a card — plain text is enough
Picking a year or month only — use Select

components.name: Dialog
use_when: Actions that need focus and shouldn't navigate away
Forms, confirmations, sharing, configuration
dont_use_when: Large panels with navigation — use Sheet/Drawer
Purely informational content — use Toast or Alert

components.name: Drawer
use_when: Mobile bottom-sheet for signal quick actions or filter controls with swipe-to-dismiss
Touch-friendly detail view of a feed item on small viewports
Progressive disclosure of report options on mobile where a centered Dialog feels cramped
dont_use_when: Desktop side panels — use Sheet
Blocking destructive confirmation — use AlertDialog
Anchored micro-interactions next to a control — use Popover

components.name: Empty State
use_when: Page, section, or inline area with no data yet
After filtering returns zero results
dont_use_when: Loading states while data fetches — use Skeleton
Error feedback after a failed action — use Alert or Toast

components.name: File Input
use_when: File upload with drag-and-drop zone inside dialogs or forms
Showing uploaded file list with remove action
dont_use_when: Simple single-file button upload — use a Button instead
Showing per-file async status outside a drop zone — use StatusSpinner in a ListItem row

components.name: Filter Chip
use_when: Filter bars above signal or mention feeds — an on/off facet like "Favourites" (icon + active + onClick)
Dropdown-style filters in the same row (PopoverTrigger asChild with showChevron)
Quick toggling of feed scopes (paid ads / owned media / news) where chips sit side by side
dont_use_when: Read-only metadata on a card — use Tag or Badge, FilterChip is interactive
Mutually exclusive view switching — use SegmentedControl
Long option lists needing search — use Combobox or Select

components.name: Filter Trigger
use_when: Inline filter selector inside a filter panel or search header (bare label + chevron, opens a dropdown)
Anywhere a filter has a label + optional current value displayed inline
Insights panel filter rows (Saved searches, Recent, Any source, etc.)
dont_use_when: Navigation — use NavItem instead
Pill-shaped filter chips — use Tag or Button ghost
Full-width row items — use NavItem
Items that expand sub-navigation — use wnav-parent with left chevron

components.name: Hover Card
use_when: Previewing a brand profile (logo, follower counts, recent signals) when hovering a brand mention
Peeking at a creator or author card from a byline in a signals feed
Showing a report or space summary on hover over a link, without navigating
dont_use_when: Short text label or hint — use Tooltip
Content the user must click or interact with (buttons, inputs) — use Popover
Touch-first or mobile flows where hover does not exist — use Popover or Sheet

components.name: Icon
use_when: Filter action buttons (paired with btn-secondary or btn-ghost)
Any UI that triggers a filter panel or filter state
dont_use_when: Sorting — use a sort icon instead
Search — use the Search icon

components.name: Input
use_when: Single-line text entry
Search, email, URL, name fields
dont_use_when: Multi-line content — use Textarea
Selection from a list — use Select or Combobox

components.name: Input OTP
use_when: Email or 2FA verification codes during sign-in
Confirming a destructive action with a short emailed code
Entering a short invite or pairing code when joining a team space
dont_use_when: Free-form text like a brand name or search query — use Input
Passwords or anything longer than ~8 characters — use Input type="password"
API keys pasted as one blob — use Input; per-character slots fight long pastes

components.name: Kbd
use_when: Showing the shortcut next to a command in the command palette
Hinting shortcuts inside Tooltip content on toolbar actions
Documenting keyboard navigation in empty states or onboarding hints
dont_use_when: Status or category labels on signals — use Badge or Tag
Interactive key remapping UI — Kbd is pointer-events-none, purely presentational

components.name: Label
use_when: Naming form fields in space settings, feed configuration, or report setup dialogs
Pairing with Checkbox/Switch/RadioGroup rows (e.g. notification toggles per signal type)
Labeling Select or Input controls in filter and creation forms
dont_use_when: Section headings inside a page or card — use heading typography, not a form label
Inline descriptive helper text under a field — use muted paragraph text

components.name: List Item
use_when: Rows in a list (people, brands, entities) with avatar + label + actions
Any interactive row that isn't a table
dont_use_when: Tabular data with multiple columns — use Table
Navigation items — use NavItem

components.name: Loader
use_when: DotsLoader for async waits — feed refreshing, signals being fetched, report list loading
ShimmerText for AI generation in progress ("Analyzing signals…", agent thinking states)
TextCursor appended to streaming AI text as it types out an insight
dont_use_when: Content with a known layout still loading — use Skeleton so the page shape stays stable
A long-running job with measurable completion — use Progress
Step/status indication on a pipeline item — use StatusSpinner

components.name: Menubar
use_when: Persistent app-level menu row in a desktop-style editor surface (e.g. report builder: File, Insert, View)
Grouped command sets with keyboard shortcuts always visible at the top of a workspace canvas
dont_use_when: Single button-triggered action list — use DropdownMenu
Primary app navigation between sections — use Sidebar or Tabs
Right-click actions on content — use ContextMenu

components.name: Nav Item
use_when: Navigation between views, sections, or settings pages
Sidebar panels (Signals, Settings, Report Builder, Insights)
Settings panel items (label + trailing icon)
Signal/feed sidebar items (count badge + label)
Report builder items (label + status indicator)
Any navigable list item that is NOT a row of data
dont_use_when: Data rows — use ListItem
Tabular data — use Table
Opening a dropdown or filtering data — use FilterTrigger

components.name: Navigation Menu
use_when: A horizontal top-bar menu where items expand into rich dropdown panels with descriptions per link
Grouped link panels on marketing or settings surfaces
dont_use_when: Primary app navigation in the shell — use Sidebar
Simple per-item dropdown actions — use DropdownMenu
Switching content panes in place — use Tabs

components.name: Pagination
use_when: Paging through bounded, countable result sets (archived reports, signal search results with known totals)
Tables of mentions where users need to jump to a specific page
dont_use_when: Continuous reverse-chronological feeds — use infinite scroll inside ScrollArea
Tiny sets that fit one screen — render the full list, no paging chrome

components.name: Popover
use_when: Date-range picker for filtering signals or report periods, anchored to a filter button
Inline settings panel for a feed widget (refresh cadence, sources) without leaving the page
Small anchored forms like "add tag to insight" or share-link options
dont_use_when: A pure action list — use DropdownMenu
Read-only hover preview of a brand or person — use HoverCard
Multi-field editing tasks or long content — use Dialog or Sheet

components.name: Progress
use_when: Showing a bulk import or backfill of signals with a known percentage
Report generation steps where completion is measurable
Quota usage bars (e.g. monthly credits consumed) in workspace settings
dont_use_when: Indeterminate waits with no known duration — use Loader (DotsLoader) or StatusSpinner
Placeholder while a card's content loads — use Skeleton

components.name: Radio Group
use_when: Mutually exclusive options where user must pick exactly one
Settings choices (e.g. "Share only / Comment / Full access")
dont_use_when: Independent toggles — use Switch or Checkbox
2 options that are view toggles — use SegmentedControl

components.name: Resizable
use_when: Split workspace views (signal feed list left, signal detail or insight editor right, user-adjustable)
Report builder layouts where users balance an outline panel against a preview pane
dont_use_when: Fixed app chrome navigation — use Sidebar (it has its own collapse behavior, not freeform resize)
Static two-column layouts users never need to adjust — use plain flex/grid

components.name: Scroll Area
use_when: A fixed-height signal feed column that scrolls independently of the page
Long filter or space lists inside popovers, dialogs, or sidebar panels that must not grow the container
Horizontal strips of report cards or media thumbnails (ScrollBar orientation="horizontal")
dont_use_when: Whole-page scrolling — let the document scroll natively
Paged, countable results where position matters — use Pagination

components.name: Segmented Control
use_when: 2–4 mutually exclusive view modes or toggles (Everyone/Only selected, Day/Week/Month)
Inside dialog body to switch between modes or filter scope
Primary page-level navigation with many items (variant="surface")
dont_use_when: More than 4 options with variant="default" — use Tabs, Select, or variant="surface"
Options with descriptions — use RadioGroup
Navigation between distinct sections — use Tabs (wtabs-pill)
Dialog footer or header — always in the body

components.name: Select
use_when: Choosing one option from a known list (5+ options)
Filter controls
dont_use_when: 2-3 options — use SegmentedControl or RadioGroup instead
Free-text search needed — use Combobox

components.name: Separator
use_when: Grouping related items in menus, dropdowns, settings panels
Visual division between sections
dont_use_when: Adding space — use padding/margin instead
Separating major page sections with their own headers — use layout, not a line

components.name: Sheet
use_when: Signal detail panel sliding in from the right while keeping the feed visible behind the overlay
Editing a space's configuration (sources, brands tracked) in a side panel
Report block inspector or insight annotation panel on desktop
dont_use_when: Mobile bottom-edge interactions with swipe-to-dismiss — use Drawer
Short confirmations or small forms — use Dialog or AlertDialog
Content that should persist alongside the page without modal overlay — use a Sidebar/Resizable layout instead

components.name: Sidebar
use_when: The app shell's primary navigation with grouped menus and an active-route indicator
Persistent workspace switcher plus per-space navigation that survives route changes and collapses to icons
Mobile navigation that should automatically become an off-canvas Sheet drawer
dont_use_when: Switching between views inside a single page — use Tabs or SegmentedControl
A temporary contextual panel (signal detail, filters) — use Sheet or Drawer
A simple flat list of links inside content — use NavItem directly without the full shell

components.name: Skeleton
use_when: Placeholder rows while a signals feed or mentions list fetches
Ghosting a report card grid or space sidebar during initial load
Reserving the shape of an insight summary block before data arrives
dont_use_when: The final layout is unknown (e.g. AI composing freeform output) — use Loader
Showing job completion percentage — use Progress

components.name: Slider
use_when: Continuous value selection (volume, opacity, price range)
Range selection with two thumbs
dont_use_when: Precise numeric input — use Input with type=number
Choosing among a small set of discrete options — use SegmentedControl or RadioGroup

components.name: Status Spinner
use_when: File upload row status
Any async operation that resolves to success/error/warning
dont_use_when: Page-level loading — use Skeleton
Button loading state — use DotsLoader or built-in Button loading prop

components.name: Switch
use_when: Immediate on/off toggle (takes effect instantly without Save)
Feature flags, notification settings, per-item enable/disable
dont_use_when: Options that need a Save action — use Checkbox + Button instead
Multi-select — use Checkbox

components.name: Table
use_when: Structured data with multiple columns and rows
Sortable, filterable data sets
dont_use_when: Simple lists — use ListItem/ListView
1-2 columns — use a list layout

components.name: Tabs
use_when: Navigation between distinct sections of a page
Switching between related views (text variant for page nav, pill for inline filters, pill-primary for main page navigation)
dont_use_when: 2-3 toggle options — use SegmentedControl
Deeply nested navigation — use sidebar

components.name: Tag
use_when: User-entered removable labels (emails, keywords, filters)
Multi-value inputs where items can be added and removed
dont_use_when: Static labels — use Badge
Single selection — use Select

components.name: Toast
use_when: Ephemeral feedback after user actions (saved, uploaded, error)
System alerts that appear in the corner and auto-dismiss
dont_use_when: Persistent warnings — use Alert
Blocking confirmations — use AlertDialog

components.name: Toggle
use_when: Icon-only formatting or view affordances in an editor toolbar (e.g. bold in a report editor)
A standalone on/off tool state in a toolbar, like snapping or compact density
dont_use_when: Filter bars above feeds — use FilterChip, the reviewed Waldo pill for that row
Settings on/off rows — use Switch with a Label
Mutually exclusive choices — use ToggleGroup type="single" or SegmentedControl

components.name: Toggle Group
use_when: Multi-select formatting clusters in an editor toolbar (bold/italic/underline together)
Toolbar single-choice icon sets, e.g. chart type (line/bar/area) on an insight visualization
dont_use_when: Primary view switching — use SegmentedControl or Tabs for page-level navigation
Independent filters over a feed — use FilterChip per facet
More than ~5 options — use Select

components.name: Tooltip
use_when: Explaining icon-only buttons
Showing keyboard shortcuts
Non-essential supplementary info
dont_use_when: Required information — put it in the UI, not a tooltip
Long text — use Popover

components.name: Wizard Dialog
use_when: Multi-step onboarding flows
Setup flows that need contextual images/previews on the right
dont_use_when: Single-step forms — use Dialog
Settings — use Dialog with tabs

brand_api_components.name: Ad Copy Card
use_when: Displaying ad creative copy (headline, body, CTA) in a card
Competitive ad teardown / creative review surfaces
dont_use_when: Generic content — use Card
A single metric — use KPI Stat Card

brand_api_components.name: Alert List Row
use_when: One row in an alerts / signals list (icon, message, timestamp)
Feed of system or brand alerts
dont_use_when: Inline ephemeral notification — use Toast
Generic list content — use ListItem

brand_api_components.name: Article Card
use_when: Displaying a press or news item mentioning a brand (media monitoring feeds)
Showing earned media coverage with source, headline, and snippet
dont_use_when: Social post (Reddit, X, TikTok) — use Comment Card instead
Internal alert or signal — use Alert List Row

brand_api_components.name: Bar Track
use_when: Ranked list of brands/entities by a single metric (SoV, reach, engagement)
Simple percentage breakdown without a reference point
dont_use_when: Metric needs a median/benchmark marker — use Benchmark Bar instead
Only one value with no comparison context — use KPI Stat Card

brand_api_components.name: Benchmark Bar
use_when: Showing brand performance vs a reference point (category median, benchmark, competitor average)
Comparing multiple metrics in a stacked layout (sentiment, SoV, engagement, etc.)
dont_use_when: Simple percentage with no reference marker — use Quote Card frequency bar instead
Binary pass/fail metric — use Badge or Alert

brand_api_components.name: Brand Column
use_when: Competitive overview grid showing brand share with supporting insight text
Height-encoded comparison of 3-5 brands where taller = higher share/metric
dont_use_when: More than 6 brands — too narrow; use a bar chart
No insight text needed — use Column Cards instead

brand_api_components.name: Code Block
use_when: Displaying preformatted code, CLI commands, or prompt text the user may need to copy
Showing multi-action copy options (e.g. "Copy prompt" + "Copy Curl") over the same content block
dont_use_when: Inline code snippets inside prose — use a bare <code> element instead
Single-line copyable values (URLs, tokens) — use CopyLink instead

brand_api_components.name: Comment Card
use_when: Displaying a social mention (Reddit, X, TikTok, Instagram, etc.)
Feed of brand mentions with platform, author, sentiment, and text
dont_use_when: Internal system alerts — use Alert List Row
Generic list content without platform context — use List Item

brand_api_components.name: Copy Link
use_when: Displaying a shareable URL the user should copy (invite link, share link, public URL)
Inside dialog bodies where a link needs to be copied with one click
dont_use_when: Editable URL fields — use Input instead
Multiple links in a list — use ListItem instead

brand_api_components.name: Depth Pill
use_when: Compact inline indicator of a depth/intensity score
Annotating an item with a small quantified badge
dont_use_when: Binary status — use Badge or Live Badge
Filtering — use FilterChip

brand_api_components.name: Drill Panel
use_when: Dashboard drill-down detail on a metric, chart point, or list row (right slide-in)
Contextual detail that should not navigate away from the dashboard
dont_use_when: Blocking confirmations or forms — use Dialog / AlertDialog
Static side content that is always visible — use Sheet (.wsheet shell)
Ephemeral feedback — use Toast

brand_api_components.name: Dual-Bar Row
use_when: Comparing the same metric between two brands/entities side by side (e.g. Nike 68% vs Adidas 52%)
Competitive snapshot rows inside a card or list
dont_use_when: Single brand metric with no comparison — use Benchmark Bar instead
More than two entities — use a chart

brand_api_components.name: KPI Stat Card
use_when: Headline metric on a dashboard (big number + label + trend delta)
Summarizing a single KPI with period-over-period change
dont_use_when: Showing more than one metric — use a grid of cards, one KPI each
Listing ranked items — use Leaderboard Row

brand_api_components.name: Leaderboard Row
use_when: Any ranked list — rank + entity + a right-hand value, one item per row
Comparing entities ordered by a metric (magnitude OR trend/momentum)
Trending items per source (hashtags, topics, subreddits) — use the trend variant + .lb-head
Multi-source comparison — one .lb column per source, composed side by side
dont_use_when: Non-ranked tabular data — use Table
A single headline metric — use KPI Stat Card
A simple percentage breakdown with a fill bar — use Bar Track

brand_api_components.name: Live Badge
use_when: Indicating a real-time / live state on a surface
Marking streaming or actively-updating data
dont_use_when: Static status label — use Badge
A depth/intensity value — use Depth Pill

brand_api_components.name: Media Tile
use_when: Displaying a social post thumbnail in a grid (image or video)
Ad creative previews in a compact grid layout
Brand owned/earned media feed grids
dont_use_when: Full article or blog post with long metadata — use Article Card
Single featured image — use a plain image with aspect-ratio

brand_api_components.name: Quote Card
use_when: Displaying a Voice-of-Customer cluster (verbatim quote + frequency)
Showing what audiences love or pain about a brand
dont_use_when: Social mention with platform/author context — use Comment Card
Generic testimonial without frequency data

brand_api_components.name: Recommendation Card
use_when: Surfacing an AI-generated strategic recommendation (comms, product, campaign, etc.)
Presenting actionable insights with supporting evidence quotes in a feed or panel
dont_use_when: Static data metric with no action — use KPI Stat Card or Benchmark Bar
Simple alert or signal without contextual evidence — use Alert List Row

brand_api_components.name: Score Ring
use_when: Showing a single 0–100 score (reputation, resonance, presence, health) where the value sits within a good/watch/at-risk range
Presenting one headline metric prominently (hero variant) or a small grid of scores (input variant)
The zone (how good/bad the number is) matters as much as the number itself
dont_use_when: The metric has no meaningful 0–100 ceiling or range — use KPI Stat Card
Comparing a value against a reference point (median, benchmark) — use Benchmark Bar
Showing a proportional breakdown of parts — use Donut Chart or Segmented Bar

brand_api_components.name: Waldo Logo
use_when: Displaying the Waldo wordmark in topbars, splash screens, or branded surfaces
dont_use_when: Generic icon usage — use a standard icon instead

charts.name: Chart Tooltip
use_when: Plotting signal volume over time for a brand or competitor feed
Visualizing sentiment or share-of-voice breakdowns inside a report
Rendering trend lines for audience or category insights in a dashboard card
dont_use_when: Showing a single completion percentage — use Progress
Displaying one headline number with a delta — use a stat card with Badge
Tabular data the user needs to sort or copy — use Table

charts.name: Chart Palette
use_when: Plotting signal volume over time for a brand or competitor feed
Visualizing sentiment or share-of-voice breakdowns inside a report
Rendering trend lines for audience or category insights in a dashboard card
dont_use_when: Showing a single completion percentage — use Progress
Displaying one headline number with a delta — use a stat card with Badge
Tabular data the user needs to sort or copy — use Table

charts.name: Segmented Bar
use_when: Showing proportional distribution of topics, themes, or categories across a brand's content
Comparing message mix between two brands side by side (one bar per brand)
Sentiment breakdown where each segment's share matters visually
dont_use_when: Comparing absolute values between entities — use Benchmark Bar or Dual-Bar Row
More than 6 segments — bar becomes unreadable; use a Donut Chart instead
Single metric with no breakdown — use a KPI Stat Card

charts.name: Column Cards
use_when: Week-over-week or period comparison of engagement metrics (posts, interactions, delta %)
When the magnitude of each period should be visually encoded by card height
dont_use_when: More than 8 periods — cards become too narrow; use a Bar Chart instead
Comparing multiple brands in the same chart — use grouped Bar Chart
Single metric with no period comparison — use KPI Stat Card
